Klarna delivers strong start to 2026 with $1bn revenue and $68m adj. operating profit
GMV reached $33.7 billion (+33% YoY), revenue $1.0 billion (+44% YoY), transaction margin dollars $389 million (+44% YoY), and adjusted operating profit $68 million, up from $3 million a year ago.
Net income: $1 million versus a net loss of $(99) million in Q1 2025
Active consumers reached 119 million (+21% YoY), and the depth of those relationships is growing as consumers use more services over time.
A $2 billion forward flow facility put in place in Q1 supports $17 billion of U.S. financing capacity, with ample runway to sustain Fair Financing's growth trajectory.
Please refer to the accompanying earnings release for more information.
With over 119 million global active Klarna users and 3.4 million transactions per day, Klarna’s AI-powered payments and commerce network is empowering people to pay smarter with a mission to be available everywhere for everything.

Adjusted operating profit $68 million Q1 2026, versus $3 million in Q1 2025
Provision for credit losses 0.55% of GMV Q1 2026, compared with 0.54% in Q1 2025
Merchants 1,075,000 As of Q1 2026, up 49% year over year
Klarna posted strong Q1 2026 results, with revenue of $1.012 billion, up 44% year over year, and gross merchandise volume of $33.7 billion, up 33%.
Active consumers increased to 119 million and merchants to 1,075,000, boosting revenue and transaction margin dollars.
Transaction Margin Dollars (TMD) was $389m in Q1, a 44% YoY growth (34% LfL) and in line with revenue growth.
Adoption of our products is increasing: The number of merchants offering Fair Financing increased to 225k (21% of merchants), up from 192k (20% of merchants) last quarter and 103k in 1Q 2025 (14% of merchants).
Transaction and Service revenue grew 29% year-over-year (21% like-for-like), with continued acceleration from Q4.
Adjusted operating income reached $68m in Q1 2026, up $65m year-on-year, with a margin of 6.7%, highlighting the earnings power of our business.
